title: CORE Session Handoff (minimum-viable)
description:
Schema for YAML front-matter of session handoff documents in .specs/planning/. Handoffs are markdown files with a YAML front-matter block; this schema validates the front-matter. The prose body is unconstrained.

Filename convention: handoff-YYYY-MM-DD.md, optionally handoff-YYYY-MM-DD-<suffix>.md for multiple sessions in one day (e.g. handoff-2026-04-20-evening.md).

File layout convention (mandatory, not machine-checked by this schema): the first line of every handoff file MUST be an HTML path comment of the form `<!-- path: .specs/planning/<filename> -->`, matching the path-identity convention used throughout .intent/papers/ and the header-line convention in src/. The YAML front-matter block (`---` fences) follows on the next line. This line exists so that the handoff file self-identifies its canonical location when extracted into context exports or viewed out of repository context — same rationale as the `# src/foo.py` header on Python source files. Enforcement is external to this schema (context-build tooling, pre-commit hook, or auditor rule); the schema documents the convention as authoritative.

Effective 2026-04-21 forward: handoffs dated before this are grandfathered — they have no front-matter and no path comment, and are not expected to validate against this schema.

date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Must match the date component of the filename.

session_focus.description: One-sentence statement of what the session was for. Not a summary of what happened — a scoping claim the prose body then accounts against.

session_length.description: Optional human-readable duration (e.g. '~2 hours', 'one long thread').

gate_status.description: Status of each A3 Gate at session close. Introduced 2026-04-21; optional for back-compatibility with pre-dated handoffs.

breakdown.description: Map of severity -> count (e.g. {WARNING: 37, INFO: 2, ERROR: 0}).

top_check_ids.description: Optional — highlight dominant check_ids (useful when one class concentrates findings, e.g. 32 modularity.needs_split of 39 total).

daemon_last_activity.description: ISO 8601 datetime of last Blackboard activity, or null if not recorded.

current_blocker.description: Short statement of the single most important thing blocking progress at session close. Use 'None immediate.' when no blocker applies.

supersedes.description: Filenames of earlier handoffs whose framing this handoff invalidates. Documentation-drift discipline: superseding is explicit, not implicit.

superseded_by.description: Filename of a later handoff that invalidated this one's framing. Added retroactively; pairs with 'supersedes' on the successor.

gate_state.description: Minimum-viable tri-state. Gate-specific language ('not demonstrated' for G1, 'not reached' for G2, 'not built' for G3) lives in the prose body, not the schema.

examples.description:
Example file layout. The schema validates the YAML front-matter object only; the path comment on line 1 and the fences around the front-matter are file-level conventions not representable in a JSON example. For illustration, a handoff file at .specs/planning/handoff-2026-04-21.md begins:

    <!-- path: .specs/planning/handoff-2026-04-21.md -->
    ---
    kind: handoff
    date: 2026-04-21
    session_focus: Add A3 Gates framing to the A3 plan.
    ...
    ---

    <prose body>

The YAML object between the fences is what validates against this schema; the example object below is that YAML deserialized.
examples.session_focus: Add A3 Gates framing to the A3 plan.
examples.current_blocker: Daemon inactive; modularity.needs_split concentration not diagnosed; G1 unproven.
examples.next_step: Option B — diagnose modularity.needs_split concentration before reactivating daemon.
